grant_title: Nita M. Lowey 21st Century Community Learning Centers (21st CCLC) Cohort 12
eligible_applicant_type: consortia of two or more eligible entities
project_area: Academic enrichment, artistic and cultural programs, literacy, STEM, career readiness, and family engagement during non-school hours for students in high-poverty and low-performing schools
demographic_requirements: Priority given to applications serving students in school districts identified for improvement and joint submissions by at least one Title I, Part A LEA and a public or private community organization
eligibility_summary: Open to LEAs, non-profits, government agencies, faith-based groups, higher education, for-profits, and consortia. Non-LEA applicants must demonstrate collaboration with a school district. Must serve students in high-poverty or low-performing schools, with priority for Title I schools and joint applications.
